description: Graph-native one-loop self-energy and vertex correction at n_max=2

self_energy_t0.ground_state_note: Continuum Theorem 4 (Paper 28) predicts Sigma(n_ext=0)=0 by vertex parity impossibility. Graph breaks this: CG projection opens couplings the continuum selection rule forbids.
self_energy_t0.algebraic_content: Diagonal entries are rational; off-diagonals contain sqrt(2), sqrt(3), sqrt(6) from CG coefficients. ALL entries are algebraic (no pi, no zeta, no log).
self_energy_t0.five_zero_eigenvalues: Kernel of dimension 5 reflects the photon propagator rank: L_1^+ has rank dim(ker(L_1)) = beta_1 = E - V + 1

vertex_correction_t0.formula: Lambda[a,b] = sum_{e',e''} G_gamma[e',e''] * V_{e'} * G_e * V_{e''}^T
vertex_correction_t0.algebraic_content: Diagonal entries rational. Off-diagonals contain sqrt(2), sqrt(3), sqrt(6). Two eigenvalues involve sqrt(145). All algebraic, no transcendentals.

anomalous_moment_t0.note: F2 is irrational (sqrt(2) from CG projection) but pi-free. The continuum Schwinger result alpha/(2*pi) does not appear -- this is a finite graph with no continuum limit.

structural_zero_check_t0.mechanism: The graph self-energy does NOT have a structural zero at the ground state. The CG projection maps Dirac (n,kappa,m_j) states onto Fock (n,l,m) nodes, opening couplings invisible to the SO(4) channel count used in the continuum selection rule.
structural_zero_check_t0.continuum_comparison: Paper 28 Theorem 4: Sigma(n_ext=0)=0 exactly by vertex parity impossibility at n_CH=0. The graph CG projection opens vertex couplings that the continuum selection rule forbids.

pi_free_certificate_t0.certificate_note: All graph-native QED quantities are algebraic (live in Q[sqrt(2), sqrt(3), sqrt(6), ...]) but NOT rational. No transcendentals (pi, zeta, log) appear. Paper 18 classification: INTRINSIC tier.

per_edge_structure.note: At t=0, every Fock edge contributes Pi_ee = 32/D to the VP where D depends on the Dirac eigenvalue product. The universal numerator 32 = 2^5 arises from the CG projection matrix squared times the diagonal propagator.
per_edge_structure.self_energy_per_edge: Sigma decomposes as sum over 3 Fock edges; each edge contribution is V_e * V_e^T weighted by G_gamma diagonal. At t=0, G_gamma = diag(1/eigenvalues_L1).

vertex_correction_t_kappa.algebraic_content: Diagonal entries: kappa=-1 states are rational; |m_j|-maximal states of kappa=-2 are rational; mixed m_j states contain sqrt(2) and sqrt(3). CG ring Q(sqrt(2), sqrt(3), sqrt(6)). kappa=+1 diagonal entries are NEGATIVE, creating one negative eigenvalue.

anomalous_moment_t_kappa.note: F2 at the physical coupling t=-1/16 is algebraic irrational in Q(sqrt(2), sqrt(3), sqrt(6)), shifted only 0.2% from the t=0 value 5*sqrt(2)/3. The CG projection introduces sqrt content that does not cancel in the trace ratio even at physical coupling.

self_energy_t_kappa.note: Self-energy Sigma is IDENTICAL at t=0 and t=-1/16 because the formula Sigma = sum G_gamma[e1,e2] * V[e1] * V[e2]^T does not involve the electron propagator G_e. Only V (CG vertex) and G_gamma (photon propagator = L_1 pseudoinverse) appear, and both are t-independent.
